text: For shallow water deposits on the land surface, a slight rise or fall in sea level can cause rapid changes in the sedimentary environment and lithofacies. In order to study the response of lithofacies characteristics to the rise and fall of sea level, taking the Cambrian system of Cangshan Mountain in Shandong Province, which has good exposure conditions and frequent lithofacies changes, as an example, the lithological characteristics of the Cambrian system in the study area were analyzed in detail through field observation, section measurement, and combined with indoor thin section identification. It is classified into seven rock phases, namely oolitic limestone phase, clastic limestone phase, marl limestone phase, gray sandstone phase, microbial limestone phase, shale phase and sandstone phase. The research suggests that the Mantou Formation in this area mainly develops tidal flat - barrier island - lagoon deposits. The Zhangxia Formation developed deposits on both confined and open platforms. Compared with the steamed bun group, the Zhang Xia group had stronger hydrodynamic conditions. The cyclical changes in water body energy, depth, light intensity and clarity affect the growth of microorganisms and the development characteristics of microbial rocks. The frequent changes of the Cambrian lithofacies in the Cangshan section of Shandong Province reflect the rise and fall of sea level and the vertical changes of the sedimentary environment. During the late period of sea level rise or the period of high sea level, open plateaus dominated, and their high-energy facies were conducive to the development of oolitic limestone and clastic limestone deposits. The low energy band under the open platform tide is conducive to the development of microbial limestone mounds and lumpy limestones. During the early stage of sea level rise or the period of sea level fall, sediments in shallow water areas dominated by limited terraces and tidal flats are mostly developed, such as argillaceous limestone and argillaceous limestone. The hydrodynamic conditions of the tidal flat environment change frequently, which can develop various types of sedimentary rock facies such as ruff limestone, argillaceous limestone, shale and sandstone

High-frequency cycle analysis is conducive to revealing a wealth of sedimentological information such as the rise and fall of sea level, sediment response, evolution of paleogeographic units and their influence on sedimentary differentiation within the basin in shallow carbonate tablelands. Based on the abundant drilling coring data in the Hengshan area of the Ordos Basin, through the analysis of rock types, typical five-stage cycle structures, etc., a high-frequency cycle study was carried out on the Mawu 6 sub-section of the Majiagou Formation of the Middle Ordovician. The research shows that: (1) Based on the nine rock types of the symbiotic combination of carbonate rocks and evaporites in the Ma Wu 6 subsection of the study area, five typical T-R single cycles were identified. Among them, cycles I, II, and III were cloud lagoon - granular beach - plaster dune deposits, and cycles IV and V were plate-salt lagoon deposits. The five-level high-frequency cycle structure profile of the skeleton well was constructed with the five single cycles as the basic units. (2) Based on the Fischer diagram established from the structural profile of the five-stage high-frequency cycle, it is believed that the Ma-5 6 subsection is in the late stage of the third-stage cycle, with 'two' fourth-stage cycles developing internally, showing a relative sea level change trend of primary sea retreat - rapid sea transgression - secondary sea retreat. (3) Based on the statistics of the thickness of the fifth-level cycles, it is believed that the sedimentary period of the first set of fourth-level cycles was in the development stage of the uplift pattern. During this period, the Hengshan Uplift, as an underwater highland, significantly isolated the seawater on the east and west sides. By the second set of quaternary cycle sedimentation stage, the filling and complementing effect weakened the influence of the Hengshan Uplift, improved the seawater circulation, and significantly reduced the salinity on both the east and west sides of the uplift. The high-frequency cycle research taking the Ma Wu 6 subsection as an example has achieved good results in the stratigraphy and sedimentology studies of the Ma Wu 6 subsection in the Hengshan area, with the expectation of further promotion and practice in the future research of the Ma Jia Gou Formation in the Ordos Basin.

The connection between high-frequency sea level fluctuations and global climate change has long been difficult to reach a unified understanding due to the confusion of many factors such as regional sea level rise and fall indicators, tectonic subsidence, rainfall and sediment age. The discussion on the driving and response mechanisms between the two important factors affecting global change - sea level rise and fall and climate change. Taking three representative boreholes in the Pearl River Delta as examples, based on the stratigraphic comparison of cyclic sedimentology and the variation of magnetic susceptibility values, the variation of geomagnetic inclination recorded by borehole SX97 was compared with the long-term variation curve of the Earth's magnetic field reconstructed from other lakes in Asia to determine the chronological framework of the sediments. Furthermore, taking the environmental magnetic parameter χARM/SIRM as an alternative indicator for high-frequency sea level fluctuations, the connection between sea level changes in the Pearl River Delta on a hundred-thousand-year scale and global climate change is discussed. The results show that the initial Marine transgressions in the Pearl River Delta occurred at approximately 9.2 ka BP, between 9.1 and 7.7 ka BP, and between 4.0 and 3.2 ka BP has two high sea level phases and several significant low sea level periods, such as 1.2-1.5,3-3.2,4.0-4.75,5.25-5.75,6.2-6.3 and 7.25-7.75 ka respectively BP is consistent with the global cooling event or the weakening of the East Asian summer monsoon, indicating that on a global scale, high-frequency sea level fluctuations have the characteristic of synchronous response to global climate change.

Based on the author's analysis results of the periodic components of the monthly average water levels at 236 stations in the Pacific Ocean, the monthly average water level sequence was fitted using the periodic components plus the linear trend term. The statistical results of the linear trend terms of these stations show that, excluding the abnormal rise and fall of sea level, the average rate of rise of the Pacific Ocean relative to sea level is 1.16 mm/a. Due to the influence of inconsistent land subsidence and other factors, the rise and fall of the Pacific Ocean relative to sea level vary significantly regionally. According to the positive or negative of the linear trend term and the geographical location of the sea area, the rise and fall of the sea level of the Pacific Ocean, including the coastal waters of China and Southeast Asia, were distinguished, and the average rise and fall rate of each sea area was calculated. The relative sea level rise and fall trends of the entire Pacific Ocean, the west coast of North America, the northern and central coastal areas of South America, most of the tropical Pacific Ocean and the coastal areas of the Japanese Islands obtained are basically consistent with the results of relevant literature. The regional average estimation result of the relative sea level in the offshore areas of East Asia in this paper is on the rise, while Barnett's estimation result tends to decline. The main reason for the inconsistency is the difference in the number of selected stations and the distribution density of water level stations

By utilizing the geochemical characteristics of elements and the geometric configuration characteristics of earthquakes, the law of sea level rise and drop in the Tarim Basin during the Cambrian - Ordovician period was studied, and the relative sea level change curve during the Cambrian - Ordovician period was established. Research has found that the Cambrian Ordovician in the Tarim Basin can be divided into 3 first-level cycles,8 second-level cycles,17 third-level cycles and multiple high-frequency oscillation cycles. Among them, the Cambrian period experienced three secondary cycles, and the Early and Middle Ordovician period experienced two cycles. Compared with the Cambrian period, the sea level rose significantly in the early Ordovician. The Late Ordovician experienced three secondary cycles: The first one was during a period of low sea level, with a relatively small amplitude; The second phase rose rapidly and reached the highest water level. The relative rise and fall of sea level control the sedimentary filling and the formation and evolution of sequences in basins. Its cyclicity is consistent with the cyclicity of basin sedimentation and can be manifested through the evolution of sedimentary facies.

The strontium isotope composition in the autogenic minerals of Marine rocks can reflect the sea level fluctuation trend during their sedimentary period. Analysis of the Ordovician 87Sr / 86Sr in the Tazhong region indicates that the sea level generally showed a downward trend during the Early Ordovician, while it continued to rise and remained above the average sea level of the Ordovician during the middle and late Ordovician. Oxygen isotope analysis indicates that the dolomites of the Lower Ordovician in the Tazhong area are mainly quasi-synbiotic dolomites deposited in a high-salinity environment during the low sea level period. The results of trace element analysis support the conclusion of isotope research. Analysis and tests show that strontium, oxygen isotopes and some trace elements all have sensitive responses to the fluctuations of sea level rise and fall.

The Cambrian reservoirs in the Weiyuan Gas Field of the Sichuan Basin belong to carbonate granular beach reservoirs, which are developed in three layers: the Lower Cambrian Longwangmiao Formation, the Middle Cambrian Gaotai Formation and the Middle and Upper Cambrian Xixiangchi Group. There are obvious differences in the characteristics of granular beach reservoirs in different layers. Research conducted on this indicates that the development and evolution of carbonate granular beach reservoirs are closely related to the three-level sea level rise and fall cycles: the granular beach reservoirs of the Longwangmiao Formation located in the early stage of the Marine retreat semi-cycle and the Xixiangchi Group located in the Marine transgresation semi-cycle are mainly composed of residual primary intergranular pores and later buried dissolved intergranular pores, with rare early exposed dissolution. The granular beach reservoirs of the Gaotai Formation, located in the late stage of the Marine retreat semi-cycle, are mainly composed of intralgranular dissolution pores formed by early exposure and dissolution. The later burial dissolution further superimposed and improved the physical properties of the early reservoirs. Therefore, the rise and fall of sea level have a significant controlling effect on the reservoir performance of carbonate granular beach reservoirs.

The Shaba Lake coast is an important type of coast. This article discusses the development process of the Shaba Lake landform along the western coast of Guangdong Province since the middle Holocene under the influence of sea level rise and fall, and draws the following conclusion: 7,000 to 5,000 years ago. The sea level once rose to +4 meters. Due to the accumulation of sediment on the continental shelf towards the land, a high protrusion was formed at the rear edge of the Jiehu Bay sand dam. Later, as the sea level gradually dropped to its current position, the receding sand wedge it formed developed into a low sea-facing sand dam and beach. 2,000 years ago, the sea level tended to be relatively stable. Nowadays, the sea level has slightly risen, and coastal erosion has occurred. The article also analyzes the coastal sediment transport mechanism under the influence of sea level rise and fall in this area, as well as the accumulation at high sea levels along the coast and the current coastal erosion phenomena.

The Devonian sea level rise and fall in the Longmenshan area is composed of the superposition of three quasi-secondary cycles: the first one contains the three sea level rise and fall cycles corresponding to the S1 to S3 sequences; The second and third ones are composed of three levels of sea level mappings, each containing six sequences from S4 to S10 and eight sequences from S11 to S18 respectively. The frequency amplitude patterns of sea level rise and fall can be identified into four types: low-frequency low-amplitude, high-frequency low-amplitude, low-frequency high-amplitude, and high-frequency high-amplitude. The entire Devonian period is characterized by the early and late low-frequency low-amplitude type, while in the middle period, it is mainly a mixture and superposition of the other three types. The comparison of the highest and lowest sea levels shows that the continents around the world have unequal timeframes, while the global sea level rise and fall protocols are similar.

The Quaternary strata in the Pearl River Delta can be divided into three sedimentary cycles, corresponding respectively to three cycles of seawater advance and retreat and three cycles of alternating warm and cold climate. The dividing fault structures at the boundary of the delta basin and within it have the same sedimentary activity attributes, and surface exploration is difficult to find the direct geological basis for their activities. The paleoclimate records of the late Pleistocene strata in this area suggest that the evolution of the Earth itself is gradually raising the current temperature, and its force far exceeds the influence of human beings.

Taking the Shidao carbonate plateau in the Xisha Islands in the northern South China Sea as the research object, the geochemical characteristics of all rock elements of 1,268 carbonate samples were analyzed. Combined with the sediological characteristics since the Early Miocene, the response relationship between them and the rise and fall of sea level was studied. The experimental results show that the paleosalinity, paleoenvironmental REDOX state, pH and sea level changes analyzed for macronutrients (Na2O/K2O, Na2O/SiO2, P2O5) and micronutrients (B/Ga, Zr/Al, Li, V) in this area are basically consistent with the conclusions obtained from petrostratigraphy and paleontology analysis, and can thus be applied to isolated seas Sedimentary sequence division of facies carbonate rock terraces; Due to the complex dolomitization process,Mg/Ca is difficult to reflect the changes in sea level. Indicators such as Na2O/K2O, Na2O/SiO2, P2O5 B/Ga, Zr/Al, Li, and V indicate that the water depth of the Sanya Formation gradually deepened in the early Miocene, which was conducive to the development of biological reefs. In the early Middle Miocene, the sea level of the second section of the Meishan Formation reached its highest value for the first time, and the reef plate expanded accordingly, forming a carbonate plateau in a relatively deep water environment, mainly composed of lagoon facies and medial beach facies. Subsequently, the water body gradually became shallower in the first section of the Meishan Formation during the late Middle Miocene and continued until the second section of the Huangliu Formation in the early late Miocene. The biological reefs were eroded by leaching and formed reef-beach facies deposits. During the late Miocene, the sea level in a section of the Yellow Stream Formation continued to rise again, reaching the second highest value in the Pliocene. It was not until the Pleistocene that the water depth gradually became shallower.

The Ordovician sea level changes in the northwest of Hunan Province were determined by integrating various geological data, including lithology, sedimentary structures, ecological types of organisms, migration of carbonate tablelands, and changes in the position of slope folds. In the northwest of Hunan Province, the changes in seawater depth, the migration of terrace edges and the alteration of slope positions show similar patterns, thus allowing the curve of sea level change to be sketched. The Lianghekou stage and Baota stage are periods of rising sea level, while the Miaopo stage and Wufeng stage are periods of falling sea level. During the 牯牛 stage, there was a small-scale change in sea level. The three sea level rise and fall cycles in this area are basically consistent with the global sea level change cycle made by Ros in 1988.

In this paper, through the study of the longitudinal distribution characteristics of brackish water fossils in the Lower Tertiary of the Bohai Bay Basin, and based on the new stratigraphic division scheme, the horizontal rise and fall curve of the early Tertiary in this area was drawn by using the comprehensive analysis method of paleontology and lithofacies. It was found that brackish water organisms appeared at the beginning of the water advance and disappeared during the maximum water advance. The rise and fall curve of the horizontal plane has no direct relationship with the global sea level rise and fall curve, that is, the rise of the horizontal plane is not affected by the rise of sea level. This thus proves that no maritime invasion occurred at this stage. The perspective of non-marine intrusion is of great significance for summarizing the theory and practice of oil and gas exploration in continental strata in China.

Foraminifera and calcareous ultrafine fossils have been discovered in the Late Pliocene-Quaternary saltwater lake sediments in northwest China. The reliability of their use as evidence of Marine transgression is questionable. The publication of the 'Comparison Table of the Tertiary System in China's Oil and Gas Regions' has made significant modifications to the old stratigraphic age values. The lake level of the early Tertiary lake basin in eastern Chinese mainland does not correspond to the global sea level rise and fall changes. Therefore, the conclusion of Marine intrusion drawn based on the old stratigraphic age values, which match the lake level with the global sea level rise and fall, lacks a reliable basis in chronostratigraphy.

The Volga Delta, located in the northern part of the Caspian Sea, is a river-controlled delta with an extremely gentle underwater slope. Over the past century, the sea level of the Caspian Sea has risen and fallen by 3.46 meters. This change can be divided into three stages: the slow decline period from 190 to 192, with an average decline rate of 1.1cm/ a; During the rapid decline period from 1930 to 1977, the average rate was 6.3 cm/ a. From 1978 to 1999, it was a period of rapid growth, with an average growth rate reaching 8.8cm/ a. During the stage of sea level decline, the Volga Delta grows by 2 to 180 square kilometers per year, which is caused by the sedimentation of river sediment and the exposure of shallow water areas above the water surface. When sea levels rise, the shallower part of the delta becomes a 'buffer zone', reducing the impact of sea level rise on the delta. Despite the rapid rise in sea levels over the past two decades or so, the Volga Delta has not witnessed common river delta phenomena such as coastal erosion, wetland loss, saltwater intrusion, and coastal flooding. By comparing with other triangles, it can be found that each major river triangle responds to the rise of sea level in different ways

By adopting a multi-disciplinary approach combining isotope geochronology, structural geology and petrology, the division and comparison framework of the pre-Nanhua metamorphic rock tectonic strata in the Three Gorges area was redefined and established, solving the long-standing and difficult problem of the division and comparison of the Middle and late Proterozoic and Mesoproterozoic tectonic strata in the north and south areas of the Huangling Dome. (2) By integrating the comprehensive research on multiple stratigraphic divisions, sequences, ecological and biostratigraphy, the division, age and comparison of rock strata from the Nanhua System to the Mesozoic era in the Three Gorges area were redefined. For the first time, the second and third sequence changes from the Sinian system to the Jurassic system in the Three Gorges area were systematically proposed, and the main tectonic geological events and the Neoproterozoic that occurred in this area from the Mesoproterozoic to the Proterozoic were pointed out The sea-level rise and fall events that have occurred since ancient times have accumulated rich data for further research on the crustal evolution of the South China Block and the sea-level rise and fall changes from the Neoproterozoic to the Mesozoic.

The issue of Marine transgression in the Early Tertiary in eastern China has been raised for many years and has attracted widespread attention. Some have proposed that the period of Marine transgression or the period of seawater influence in the Early Tertiary in eastern China 'happened to be the main oil-producing period of various oil-bearing basins. The scope of Marine transgression or the influence of Marine water is closely related to beneficial oil-forming zones, and it is recognized that 'clarifying the temporal and spatial distribution of each Marine transgression is of great significance for delineating beneficial oil and gas exploration zones and pre-measuring oil and gas prospects.' The chart shows that the vertical and horizontal distribution of Marine transgression is closely related to the oil and gas resources of the lower third system. The issue of Marine encroachment is no longer merely a matter of theoretical exploration; it involves the direction and deployment of oil and gas exploration that consume a huge amount of capital. Therefore, it has naturally drawn the attention of foreign oil and gas companies. The issue of early Tertiary Marine transgressions in eastern China was initially determined based on fossils such as foraminifera, flagellates, mesozoids, and calcareous superdeposits, and gradually expanded to the field of sedimentology. Some sediment analysts have analyzed Marine facies minerals such as aquamarine, elemental boron content, boron yield value, and carbon and oxygen isomers. Even based on the variation of the content of clay minerals such as ilite and kaolingite, which support the observation points of Marine transgressions, the discussion has subsequently expanded to the entire geological domain. Recently, some academic works have proposed that the rise and fall of lake levels in some lake basins in eastern China during the Early Tertiary period basically correspond to or are consistent with the rise and fall of global sea levels, once again presenting evidence of Marine transgression. Based on the data accumulated over many years from oil and gas exploration in the northwest salt lake area and the Bohai Bay Basin, the author raises objections for discussion among peers. The author believes that the sea transgression was magnified and expanded. Among the lake basins of the early third period classified as sea transgression, many were continental saltwater lakes, not all of which were sea transgression lakes.

This paper discusses the sea level changes from the Late Ordovician to the early Silurian and the demarcation of the Ordovician/Silurian boundary through the research of biostratigraphy, chemical stratigraphy and sequence stratigraphy. Studies on the morphology, differentiation and abundance of graptolites, brachiopods and radiolarians have shown that: From the late Ordovician to the early Silurian in the Yangtze Basin, on the whole, the sea level underwent a change from rising to falling and then rising again. The maximum stage of sea level rise occurred in the lower part of the Tangyagraptus typicus subzone, and at the top of the Guanyinqiao Formation, the sea level dropped to the lowest point. At the bottom of the Wufeng Formation - Longmaxi Formation in the Goujiaya and Datangkou sections, the abundances of trace elements such as Ni, V, Sc, Rb, Sr and Ba, as well as the abnormal changes of rare earth element Ce, have similar regularity and can be divided into five cycles, corresponding respectively to the lower and upper parts of the D.complexus zone. The lower part of the P.pacificus zone, the upper part of the P.pacificus zone to the N. Ojsuensis zone, and the N.persculptus zone to the P. acinatus zone. On these two sections, the abundance changes of radiolarians also show similar patterns, which are largely consistent with the changes in the morphology, differentiation and abundance of graptolites and brachiopods, and can be compared with the five fluctuations of sea level in North America and Africa during the same period. Based on this, it is pointed out that the above five sea level cycles represent the global sea level changes from the late Ordovician to the early Silurian. In the first four cycles, the period of each fluctuation was 0.75 to 1Ma, which was similar to the period of Grade IV sea level change caused by glacial activity (0.1 to 1Ma). Based on this, it can be further inferred that there were four grade IV sea level changes in the late Ordovician (from the D.complexus to the N. Ojsuensis zone). Based on the indicative significance of radiolarians, graptolites, brachiopods, spongy bone needles and lithology for the paleoenvironment, it is believed that the bottom grayish-green mudstone section of the Wufeng Formation was formed at a water depth of approximately 60-100 meters, and the water depth of the Guanyinqiao Formation was about 50-80 meters. During the maximum Marine intrusion period of the Late Ordovician (corresponding to the lower part of the T. tupicus subzone), the water depth was approximately 400 meters. The minimum water depth during the deposition of radiolarian-containing siliceous rocks in the Wufeng Formation was approximately 200 meters. Based on the above five cycles of sea level change, the variation range of sea level in each cycle was calculated: In the first cycle, the range of sea level change was roughly 20 to 120 meters. In the second, third and fourth cycles, the sea level changes are 80 to 130 meters, approximately 150 meters and 50 to 250 meters respectively. The sea level rise and fall range of the fifth cycle is greater than 200 meters. The geological markers of the chronostratigraphic boundaries demarcated in accordance with the requirements of the 'International Stratigraphic Guidelines' are not obvious, which is not conducive to identification and does not fully conform to the rhythm of the Earth's evolution.

From the transition periods of the Sinian/Cambrian to the Ordovician/Silurian, Frasian/Famenian, Permian/Triassic, Triassic/Jurassic, and the Cretaceous/Tertiary, the geological events that occurred in various regions follow the following patterns: The geological history time spanned by the C and O isotope anomaly events is longer than or basically equal to the time spanned by the boundary clay layer or the extinction event of organisms. (2) Marine transgressions usually occur immediately after other geological events and are basically consistent with the recovery after the extinction of species. (3) The period before the maritime invasion was the peak of the event, followed by a relatively calm geological history period. (4) The time spans of the adjacent geological historical transition periods mentioned above are 105Ma, 66Ma, 122Ma, 45Ma and 140Ma respectively. It can be seen that geological events occur alternately in long and short periods, and the sum of two adjacent long and short periods is basically equal (66Ma+122Ma=188Ma, 45 mA +140Ma=185Ma). (5) Near the dynastic boundaries of the Permian/Triassic, Cretaceous/Tertiary and other periods, the scale of geological events is even larger. Not only have there been extinctions of biological clusters, but also global chemical anomaly events have occurred. These laws indicate that the evolution of the Earth has rhythmicity. Global maritime transgressions may be a turning point for the Earth's transition from an unbalanced state to a balanced one, or a turning point in the transitional period of geological history. Therefore, it is pointed out that when demarcating the boundaries of chronostrata, the significance of natural events that reflect the rhythm of the Earth's evolution, especially Marine transgression events, should be fully considered. Based on the irreversibility of biological evolution, the rhythm of Earth's evolution, and the patterns among geological events during historical transition periods in various regions, it is suggested that when demarcating the boundaries of chronostratigraphic units, the biostratigraphic division should be taken as the foundation, closely combined with natural events, and used as an auxiliary marker. When demarcating the boundaries of the Ordovician/Silurian system, it is advocated to use the first appearance of the pen stone N. Persculptus as a biological marker and the geological events that occurred basically simultaneously as auxiliary markers, so as to facilitate extensive global comparison. Given that the geological records left by different regions are not exactly the same, the comparison methods of the Ordovician/Silurian boundary are different: on the boundary layer profile, the first appearance of N. Persculptus is used as the biological marker, the basically consistent maritime intrusion event is used as the auxiliary physical marker, and the biological and chemical markers are used as the auxiliary biological and chemical markers. In regions where graptolites are highly underdeveloped, Marine invasion events, extinction events or chemical anomaly events are used as auxiliary markers, and other types of fossils (such as odontoids) that are closest to them and have regional comparability are used as biological markers for boundary comparison. For regions with significant stratigraphic deficiencies caused by sea retreat events, the physical markers for demarcating the boundaries are the sea retreat/intrusion events, and the beginning of the corresponding intrusion events serves as the dividing line for the Ordovician/Silurian system.

The Upper Miocene reef complex in Mallorca is a tertiary sedimentary sequence that reveals the response of carbonate deposits to high-frequency sea-level change cycles. This reef complex demonstrates a composition format of multi-level high-frequency unit superposition ranging from four to seven levels, with its building blocks being S-shaped units bounded by unconformity rather than quasi-sequence. These S-shaped units are superimposed into S-shaped unit hierarchies, S-shaped unit hierarchies are superimposed into 'S-shaped unit hierarchies groups', and these hierarchies groups are superimposed into 'hierarchies groups'. All these proliferative units have the characteristics of sedimentary sequences, but they are different from the Exxon model and other carbonate sequence formation models. The cause of these deviations lies in the changes in the amount of carbonate produced due to the fluctuations in sea level. When there is no significant subsidence, the fluctuations in sea level determine the changes in the accommodative space. All these high-frequency sequences are composed of four system domains :(1) Lower system domain: Formed during the initial ascending period of the sea level change cycle, it consists of the progressive reef phase and the open shelf phase that thins into the basin; (2) Filling system domain: Formed during the period of sea level rise, it is composed of thin layers of lagoons to open shelf phases, and does not show the characteristic of migration to the continental phase. (3) High-level system domain: Formed at the high end of the sea level change cycle, it is composed of progressive reef facies and open shelf facies that thins into the basin, lacking or only having thin lagoon sedimentary layers. (4) Receding system domain: Formed during the period of sea level descent, it is composed of thin lower superreef phases. These reef phases gradually transform into an open shelf condensation layer within the basin, and the condensation layer is comparable to the erosion surface at the top of the platform. Between these system domains, two boundary surfaces can be identified based on lithologic phase changes. That is, the erosion surface at the top of the receding system domain and the lower superplane at its base merge into one condensation layer within the basin. The sequence boundary corresponding to the lowest point of the sea level change cycle is an erosion surface towards the land, while it continues in a considerable condensed layer towards the basin.
